Description: A lone unclassified brand candidate violates the shared ambiguity schema. selectBrandObservation returns AMBIGUOUS for single results, which the schema rejects, causing INVALID_RESPONSE instead of usable uncertainty.
Plausible Alternative: Use distinct state (UNCLASSIFIED), allow AMBIGUOUS with zero alternates, return LOW_CONFIDENCE, or fix selector/schema contradiction before measurement.
Verdict Direction: HARDEN the schema composition, KEEP the evidence/rule boundary; Fix required.

Description: High test count (618) lacks composition tests to catch cross-layer failures; architecture tests rely on static searches.
Plausible Alternative: Add contract tests at production boundaries, run synthetic cases through analyzer, or use dependency-graph enforcement.
Verdict Direction: HARDEN test composition; KEEP current breadth for regression protection.

Description: Circular dependencies exist in the plan (issues #6, #16, #39, #40) if treated as indivisible gates. Additionally, PR body claims 10 domain-only synthetic cases, while the merged index contains 11.
Plausible Alternative: Use a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of deliverables, vertical per-field slices, or explicit partial-order milestones. Correct documentation to match code reality.
Verdict Direction: KEEP evidence-before-expansion; HARDEN the dependency model; Refine plan; Minor correction.

Description: Governance lane is not executable as written; Design Review Checklist and Operator Trust Policy are truncated. Truncated issues and broken links make documentation an active prerequisite.
Plausible Alternative: Replace broken policy content, add parallel truncation checks, and prioritize documentation integrity (#28) before or alongside policy replacement (#27).
Verdict Direction: REVISIT strict lane ordering; keep lanes conceptually separate; Remediate integrity.

Description: Implementation excludes timing and government-warning execution required by ADR 0009; ADR remains historically stale.
Plausible Alternative: Amend ADR 0009 to reflect Slice 3 reality.

Description: Calling it a 'six-rule profile' may imply broader automation than current evidence supports, as half the profile cannot execute.
Plausible Alternative: Split profile into executable vs. dependency checks or publish executable-coverage counts.
Verdict Direction: KEEP the explicit not-run model; HARDEN profile descriptions.

Description: Expensive Tesseract worker creation per request lacks queueing, timeouts, or concurrency control required for deployment. Tesseract reproducibility across platforms is unproven.
Plausible Alternative: Implement worker pooling, containerize OCR runtime, use bounded timeouts, or add tolerance rules when actual-content evidence exists.
Verdict Direction: KEEP local-only OCR baseline; HARDEN before public upload deployment; Deferred to operational scaling.

Description: Lack of server-side persistence allows histories to be forked. Tokens are reusable bearer capabilities that fail after server restart and lack global serialization/authenticated identity.
Plausible Alternative: Label as 'portable operator record', add server persistence (#17), or implement globally serialized audit logs.
Verdict Direction: KEEP the prototype separation; HARDEN language; REVISIT replay semantics; Accepted for current slice.

Description: Only one real label demonstrates OCR behavior; the corpus cannot yet estimate general accuracy or platform variation.
Plausible Alternative: Increase diversity of independently sourced real labels.

Description: Conservative selector favors ambiguity over false observations for brand candidates without specific designator signals.
Plausible Alternative: Measure false-observed burden, add local brand-region classifier later, or require explicit operator confirmation.
Verdict Direction: KEEP conservative uncertainty; HARDEN the state model; REVISIT positive-signal vocabulary.

Description: Independent status model prevents false passes but lacks corpus-wide distribution data and may cause operator friction.
Plausible Alternative: Keep independent statuses but add a bounded reason vocabulary and measure correction time in issue #38.
Verdict Direction: KEEP the boundary; HARDEN reason semantics and measurement.

Description: Unavailable entries provide no executable behavior; low-res derivative lacks precise parent-child schema representation.
Plausible Alternative: Move unavailable records to acquisition backlog, report executable counts separately, or extend manifest parent model.
Verdict Direction: KEEP gap recording; REVISIT measurement inclusion; HARDEN execution and parent semantics.

Description: Mechanisms for machine identity and authorization are distinct but prone to misunderstanding by operators.
Plausible Alternative: Persist server-issued records, use signed compact envelopes, or keep deterministic HMAC with explicit documentation.
Verdict Direction: KEEP the three distinctions; HARDEN with an ADR and key-id plan.

Description: Large contract relative to the empirical base (one real label) may encode implementation assumptions too early.
Plausible Alternative: Keep v1 experimental, separate immutable metadata from evaluation scenarios, or use smaller per-fixture files.
Verdict Direction: KEEP a versioned catalogue; REVISIT expectation shape after first harness.

Description: Synthetic cases bypass OCR/image processing; author-stated count in PR (10) differs from merged index (11).
Plausible Alternative: Separate synthetic cases from accuracy metrics, render synthetic images for OCR path, or prioritize public artwork.
Verdict Direction: KEEP synthetic cases; HARDEN category labeling.

Description: Regex-based screening cannot prove pixels exclude all personal data; over-cropping risks making benchmarks unrealistic.
Plausible Alternative: Adopt two-person approval checklist, define allowed business content explicitly, and obtain legal confirmation.
Verdict Direction: KEEP privacy-first inclusion; HARDEN approval evidence and policy.

Description: Sound semantics but lacks automated accessibility audits (axe) and measurement of operator friction due to ambiguity.
Plausible Alternative: Add automated scanning, conduct moderated operator walkthroughs, or begin local friction metrics.
Verdict Direction: KEEP UI semantics; HARDEN accessibility and operator-friction evidence.
